activities_projects.title: Freedom Leaders Collage
activities_projects.objective: Identify key leaders of the Indian National Movement
steps: Collect pictures of leaders like Mahatma Gandhi, Jawaharlal Nehru, and Subhash Chandra Bose
Paste them on chart paper to create a collage
Label each leader with their name and one famous slogan they gave
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes major personalities involved in the freedom struggle

activities_projects.title: Satyagraha Map Activity
activities_projects.objective: Locate early movements led by Gandhiji
steps: Locate Champaran (Bihar), Kheda (Gujarat), and Ahmedabad (Gujarat) on the map
Mark them with different colored dots
Write the year each movement took place next to the dot
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the geographical start of Gandhian Satyagraha
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of locations and dates (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dandi March Storyboard
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the sequence of the Salt March
steps: Divide the paper into four sections
Draw four scenes: 1. Gandhiji leaving Sabarmati, 2. Walking with followers, 3. Reaching Dandi, 4. Picking up salt
Write a one-sentence caption for each scene
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recall the process and significance of the Salt Satyagraha
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct sequence of events and creativity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: National Symbols Identification
steps: Draw the 'Swaraj Flag' designed by Gandhiji in 1921
Identify the three colors and the symbol (spinning wheel) in the center
Write what the spinning wheel represented (self-help)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understanding the visual tools of unity
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the flag design and meaning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Freedom Struggle Timeline
activities_projects.objective: Arrange major events in chronological order
steps: Draw a straight horizontal line
Place the following events: Rowlatt Act (1919), Non-Cooperation (1921), Civil Disobedience (1930), and Quit India (1942)
Add a small icon for each (e.g., a stick for the Salt March)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Builds a basic sense of historical chronology
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct order and dating of events (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Patriotic Slogan Banner
steps: Select three slogans (e.g., 'Inquilab Zindabad', 'Do or Die', 'Jai Hind')
Write them in bold letters on a banner
Mention which leader gave which slogan
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects famous words to their respective leaders
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct matching of slogans and leaders (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Idea of Satyagraha Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand the core concept of Gandhi’s philosophy
steps: Write 'SATYAGRAHA' in the center of the page
Draw two bubbles coming out of it: 'Truth' and 'Non-violence'
Write one simple sentence explaining why Gandhiji believed in non-violence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the basic moral foundation of the movement

activities_projects.title: Role-Play: Boycott of Foreign Cloth
activities_projects.objective: Experience the methods of the Non-Cooperation Movement
materials: Props (like a piece of Khadi cloth)
steps: Work in pairs to act out a small scene at a shop
One student plays a shopkeeper selling foreign cloth, the other plays a protester asking to buy Indian Khadi
Discuss why people were burning foreign clothes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the economic aspect of the boycott
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and understanding of the 'Swadeshi' concept (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Freedom Movement Word Search
activities_projects.objective: Reinforce key terms from the chapter
steps: Find 10 words hidden in a grid: Gandhi, Swaraj, Salt, Rowlatt, Khadi, Congress, Satyagraha, Dandi, Purna, Swaraj
Circle the words once found

activities_projects.title: Bharat Mata Coloring Activity
activities_projects.objective: Identify the early allegory of India
materials: Outline of Abanindranath Tagore’s Bharat Mata
steps: Color the image of Bharat Mata
Label the four things she holds in her hands: Cloth, Beads, Book, and Sheaves of Grain
Write what she represents in one word (Nation)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the personification of the Indian nation
